annotations.label: Minor of a matrix

anchor.snippet: a minor of a matrix A is the determinant of some smaller square matrix generated from A by removing one or more of its rows and columns

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Matrix.submatrix` for reindexing/extracting submatrices but no named `minor` definition; one would take `det` of a submatrix.

annotations.label: First minor (i,j)

anchor.snippet: the minor of the entry in the i -th row and j -th column

annotations.note: The construction `A.submatrix i.succAbove j.succAbove` appears in `det_succ_row`, but Mathlib has no named `firstMinor` (i,j) definition.

annotations.label: (i,j) cofactor

anchor.snippet: The ( i , j ) cofactor is obtained by multiplying the minor by

annotations.note: Cofactors appear only inline in the Laplace-expansion formula `(-1)^(i+j) * det (A.submatrix i.succAbove j.succAbove)`; no standalone `cofactor` definition exists.

annotations.label: Computing M_{2,3} and C_{2,3}

anchor.snippet: To compute the minor M 2,3 and the cofactor C 2,3

annotations.note: No worked numerical example of a first minor/cofactor exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: k × k minor

anchor.snippet: A k × k minor of A , also called minor determinant of order k of A

annotations.note: k×k minors are expressible as `det (A.submatrix r c)` for injections `r, c : Fin k → _`, but no named `Matrix.minor` concept exists.

annotations.label: Minor of order zero

anchor.snippet: The minor of order zero is often defined to be 1

annotations.note: `det_fin_zero` gives `det = 1` for the empty matrix, which is the order-zero minor convention, though not framed as a minor.

annotations.label: Minor indexed by I and J

anchor.snippet: The minor

annotations.note: Mathlib's `submatrix` is reindex-based rather than index-set-based; no `[A]_{I,J}` notation/definition is present.

annotations.label: Complement of a minor

anchor.snippet: is formed by the determinant of the matrix A from which all the rows

annotations.note: No definition of the complementary minor (deleting the chosen rows/columns) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laplace cofactor expansion

anchor.snippet: the determinant of A , denoted det( A ) , can be written as the sum of the cofactors of any row or column of the matrix multiplied by the entries that generated them

annotations.note: `Matrix.det_succ_row` and `Matrix.det_succ_column` give the Laplace cofactor expansion along any row or column of an (n+1)×(n+1) matrix.

annotations.label: Cofactor matrix

anchor.snippet: The matrix formed by all of the cofactors of a square matrix A is called the cofactor matrix

annotations.note: The cofactor matrix isn't given a name; only its transpose `Matrix.adjugate` is defined (via Cramer's rule rather than minors).

annotations.label: Inverse via adjugate

anchor.snippet: Then the inverse of A is the transpose of the cofactor matrix times the reciprocal of the determinant of A

annotations.note: `Matrix.inv_def` states `A⁻¹ = A.det⁻¹ʳ • A.adjugate`, matching the adjugate inverse formula.

annotations.label: Adjugate matrix

anchor.snippet: The transpose of the cofactor matrix is called the adjugate matrix

annotations.note: `Matrix.adjugate` is defined via the Cramer map and documented as the transpose of the cofactor matrix.

annotations.label: Generalized inverse formula via minors

anchor.snippet: The above formula can be generalized as follows

annotations.note: Mathlib has no expression of the inverse in terms of higher-order minors of the matrix.

annotations.label: Rank characterized by nonzero minor

anchor.snippet: then there exists at least one non-zero r × r minor, while all larger minors are zero

annotations.note: No characterization of `Matrix.rank` via existence of a nonzero r×r minor is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Principal minor

anchor.snippet: If A is square and I = J , then [ A ] I , J is called a principal minor

annotations.note: No `principalMinor` definition appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Leading principal minor

anchor.snippet: the principal minor [ A ] I , J is called a leading principal minor

annotations.note: No `leadingPrincipalMinor` definition appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Basic minor

anchor.snippet: A basic minor of a matrix with rank r is an r × r minor with nonzero value

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a `basicMinor`.

annotations.label: Sylvester's criterion application

anchor.snippet: the leading principal minors can be used to test for positive definiteness and the principal minors can be used to test for positive semidefiniteness

annotations.note: Sylvester's criterion is not present in `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PosDef.lean` or elsewhere.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Binet generalized formula for minors of a product

anchor.snippet: are special cases of the following general statement about the minors of a product of two matrices

annotations.note: Only the square-case `Matrix.det_mul` is in Mathlib; the general Cauchy–Binet formula for minors of a product is not present.

annotations.label: Minors as components of exterior power

anchor.snippet: the k -minors of a matrix are the entries in the k -th exterior power map

annotations.note: While Mathlib has exterior powers and even `exteriorPower.cofactorLinear` in a stably-free setup, no result identifies k-minors of a matrix with entries of its k-th exterior power.

annotations.label: 2 × 2 minors via wedge product

anchor.snippet: are −13 (from the first two rows), −7 (from the first and last row), and 5 (from the last two rows)

annotations.note: No worked wedge-product computation of 2×2 minors is present.

annotations.label: Adjunct (alternative term)

anchor.snippet: instead of cofactor the term adjunct is used

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `adjugate` exclusively and does not introduce `adjunct` terminology.
